title: Cirrus: Add reverse-deps. test to verify proxy with ostree-rs-ext
body:
This does reverse-dependency testing, verifying `proxy.go` using the ostree-rs-ext Rust code's unit tests.

Based on #1781 by @cgwalters

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @cgwalters PTAL, this should be ready to go.

***Note:*** Ignore all the `...but their only_if conditions are different` annotations, they're harmless - just warning that bypass of a parent could cause child artifact-retrieval failure.  Since we're not passing artifacts, there is no problem.

Great.  I remembered that rust builds are fairly intensive.  If the ~7min time becomes a problem, there are some build-caching options available.  Unless you object, I'm content to leave this as-is until the runtime proves problematic (caching adds a lot of complexity).

Thanks for checking Miloslav.  Yeah, that was in lieu of running in PRs.  I think rather than try and predict where a test-failure will be useful, let's just run it the same as any other skopeo test.  It's possible that any PR or merge could break it, so this way we'll know.  

We've already got cirrus-cron daily running on `main` here, we can keep an eye out in case it breaks on `main` :grinning:
